summary: A Goodreads author profile for Andrew Carnegie, highlighting his background as a Scottish-born American steel industrialist and philanthropist. The page lists his major works, including 'The Gospel of Wealth,' and features a few of his notable quotes on the value of libraries, the importance of observing actions over words, and the necessity of self-motivation.

principles.statement: The wealthy have a duty to use their fortunes to improve society.
principles.rationale: He believed that wealth should be given away to charities, foundations, and universities for the benefit of the public, rather than hoarded.

principles.statement: Libraries are the greatest benefit a community can provide to its people.
principles.rationale: They act as a continuous, reliable source of enrichment and knowledge for the public.
principles.source_quote: A library outranks any other one thing a community can do to benefit its people. It is a never failing spring in the desert.

principles.statement: Self-motivation is a prerequisite for rising above mediocrity.
principles.rationale: Without the ability to motivate oneself, even the most impressive natural talents will not prevent a person from achieving only mediocre results.
principles.source_quote: People who are unable to motivate themselves must be content with mediocrity, no matter how impressive their other talents.

heuristics: Judge people by their actions rather than their words.

signature_quotes.text: A library outranks any other one thing a community can do to benefit its people. It is a never failing spring in the desert.
As I grow older, I pay less attention to what men say. I just watch what they do.
People who are unable to motivate themselves must be content with mediocrity, no matter how impressive their other talents.

anti_patterns.description: Relying on talent alone without self-motivation.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It inevitably leads to mediocrity, regardless of how impressive the underlying talents might be.

anti_patterns.description: Trusting what people say over what they do.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Words can be deceptive or empty; actions are a truer indicator of a person's character and intent.
